entry.title: NR4 Canada Report in SAP DRC – Available for On-Premise and Private Edition, soon in Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

Canadian tax compliance just got a significant upgrade in SAP. We are excited to announce that the NR4 withholding tax report for Canada is now available in SAP S/4HANA 2023 and SAP S/4HANA 2025, released via SAP Note 3748353. In addition, it is planned to be delivered in SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS02 (October 2026) and in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2608.2 (October 2026).

The report is delivered as part of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) — the same solution that already hosts the T4A-NR report — giving Canadian customers a consistent, unified experience for non-resident withholding tax reporting.

This new report directly addresses a long-standing need expressed by our Canadian customers and complements the existing T4A-NR report that was already available in SAP.

What Is the NR4 Report?

The NR4 – Statement of Amounts Paid or Credited to Non-Residents of Canada is a form mandated by the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA). It must be filed by Canadian payers who have paid or credited amounts — such as dividends, royalties, rental income, management fees, or other passive income — to non-residents of Canada during a calendar year.

For every non-resident recipient, a corresponding NR4 slip must be issued, and an NR4 summary must be submitted to the CRA. This is a critical reporting obligation under the Income Tax Act of Canada and is distinct from the T4A-NR, which covers payments to non-residents for services rendered in Canada (such as acting or performance fees).

What's New: NR4 Report in SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

With the delivery of this new functionality, SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud now supports the automated generation of NR4 slips and summary directly from the withholding tax postings recorded in the system.

Key capabilities of the new NR4 report include:

  • Extraction of withholding tax data posted against non-resident vendors and business partners
  • Income type classification according to CRA-defined NR4 income codes (e.g., dividends, royalties, rents, management fees)
  • Currency conversion handling for amounts paid in foreign currencies, in line with CRA requirements
  • Country of residence derivation from business partner master data
  • Tax treaty code mapping based on the non-resident's country of tax residency
  • Gross income and withholding tax amounts per recipient, per income type
  • Output in the format required for CRA electronic filing

How It Complements the T4A-NR

We have heard your feedback. Many of our Canadian customers highlighted that while the T4A-NR report covered payments to non-residents for services, there was no equivalent SAP-delivered solution for the NR4, which covers passive income payments to non-residents. The NR4 report fills this gap.

 

Feature

T4A-NR

NR4

Payment type

Services rendered in Canada

Passive income (dividends, royalties, rents, etc.)

Recipient

Non-residents providing services

Non-residents receiving passive income

CRA form

T4A-NR slip

NR4 slip

Available in SAP DRC

Yes

Now available

 

Configuration: What You Need to Set Up

At the Business Partner Level

A key prerequisite is the correct setup of the Business Partner master data. For the NR4 report to correctly identify and extract non-resident payees, the following must be maintained at the business partner level:

  • Country of residence / tax country: The country where the non-resident is tax-resident must be maintained. This drives both the reporting and the applicable treaty withholding tax rate.
  • Withholding tax data: The relevant withholding tax type and code for Canada NR4 must be assigned to the business partner, including the non-resident's tax identification number (e.g., the Social Insurance Number, Business Number, or foreign TIN as applicable).
  • NR4 income type code: The CRA income type code (e.g., code 09 for rental income, code 11 for royalties, code 13 for management fees, code 25 for dividends) must be configured and associated with the applicable withholding tax type/code combination.

For full configuration details at the business partner level, refer to the SAP Help Portal: Business Partner Configuration for NR4 – SAP Help Portal

In SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

The NR4 report is executed from within SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, the same way you already run the T4A-NR report. It allows you to:

  1. Select the reporting year and company code(s)
  2. Filter by withholding tax type relevant to NR4
  3. Generate the output for review, correction, and submission to the CRA

For the full functional documentation of the report, refer to the SAP Help Portal: NR4 Withholding Tax Report for Canada – SAP Help Portal

 

Availability Summary

 

EditionReleaseAvailability
SAP S/4HANA On-Premise & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition2023Available – SAP Note 3748353
SAP S/4HANA On-Premise & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition2025Available – SAP Note 3748353
SAP S/4HANA On-Premise & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition2025 FPS02Planned – October 2026
SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition2608.2Planned – October 2026 

 

Getting Started

If you are on SAP S/4HANA 2023 or 2025 on-premise, implement SAP Note 3748353 to activate the NR4 report. Ensure your Basis team has applied the note and that your Finance/Tax team reviews the business partner configuration prerequisites before the next reporting cycle.

If you are on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the functionality will be available with the 2608.2 release in October 2026 — no manual note implementation required.

 

We Want Your Feedback

The NR4 report was developed in direct response to customer demand, and your input continues to shape what we build next. If you have questions, configuration challenges, or suggestions for future enhancements, we invite you to share your feedback through our dedicated Work Zone community:

Canada ERP Localization – Work Zone

We look forward to hearing from you.

entry.title: Assembly Subcontracting with Sales Order Stock (E) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: 7DW
entry.content:
Business Case

ABC Scooter Company is executing a Customer Order of 100 Scooters with Matte Black Panel. The Body Panel is manufactured specifically for the specific customer order (Sales Order Stock E), while Primer and Paint are standard consumables issued from Regular Plant Stock. The subcontractor paints the body panel and returns the finished Painted Black Body Panel back into Sales Order Stock (E).

By using Sales Order Stock (E) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the company ensures that all materials and subcontracting activities are strictly tied to the individual customer order, enabling precise cost tracking, eliminating cross-consumption.

Other applicable industries:

  • Industrial Machinery (custom components outsourced for specialist machining),
  • Medical Devices (patient-specific items outsourced for sterile packaging),
  • Electronics (customer-specific assemblies outsourced for specialist coating),
  • Apparel & Fashion (sales-order fabric sent to CMT vendor for cut-make-trim).

What changed in 2608:

  • The Subcontracting PO can now contain BOM with Components both from Regular inventory stock and Special stock (Sales Order Stock: E) in the same order.
  •  No stock type conversion required: E transfers directly to F at the vendor.
  • Full MTO support: materials and costs tied strictly to the individual customer sales order no cross-consumption.
  • No financial document at Goods Issue: the FI/accounting posting occurs only at Goods Receipt.

For scope item 7DW, the stock type transitions are:

  • Sales Order Stock component (e.g. RM027): E (at producer)  →  F Stock (at vendor)  →  Consumed on GR
  • Regular Plant Stock component: O (at producer)  →  O at vendor  →  Consumed on GR
  • Finished product (e.g. SG035): Received back into Sales Order Stock (E) on GR

Important: No financial or accounting document is generated when components are issued to the subcontractor (Goods Issue). This is a stock-to-stock transfer only no consumption has occurred yet. The financial posting is triggered only at Goods Receipt.

  • Example Master Data: SG035(main material), Components: RM027(Sales Order Stock)
  • In the material Master of RM027: Go to MRP4 and set the individual/Collective requirement as 1(Individual)
  • Note: The Individual/Collective Requirement = 1 (Individual) setting on MRP4 is mandatory. Without it, MRP will not plan the component against the specific sales order it will be treated as pooled stock and the Sales Order Stock (E) link will be lost.

  • For SG035: In the MRP2 Tab: maintain the following.

  • Go to the tab MRP4 and set the individual/Collective requirement as 1(Individual)

Process Steps:

Step 1: Create Sales Order

  • Create a Sales Order in the App: Manage Sales Order(F1873).

  • Ensure the Storage Location, shipping point is determined rightly
  • Save the Sales Order document and note down the number.

Step 2: Ensure Components are Available

  • Before running MRP, ensure the required components are available in the correct stock type:
  • Note: Either procure the components via a standard purchase order or production order referencing the sales order (so they land in Sales Order Stock E), or ensure sufficient stock already exists in the respective stock type before planning. Do not start MRP planning until the component stock is confirmed available.

Step 3: Run MRP

  • Schedule MRP Run in the background in the App: Schedule MRP Run(F1339) or
  • Go to the App: Manage Material Coverage(F0251) and run the MRP directly
  • Select the Material and click on details and Run MRP directly here.  Post MRP Run, for the respective Sales Order, a Purchase Requisition should be created. Note down the Purchase Requisition number.

Step 4: Create Subcontracting Purchase Order

  • Go to the App: Process Purchase Requisitions(F1048).
  • Enter the Purchase Requisition or filter based on your criteria, select the Purchase Requisitions and Assign the Supplier, should be determined based on the Purchase InfoRecords.

  • Then click Create Purchase Order, the Purchase Order Simulation should be displayed.
  • In the Items Table, the Account Assignment = M (Sales Order) and Item Category = L (Subcontracting) must be pre-determined. Note: Account Assignment M = Make-to-Order / Sales Order. This ties the subcontracting PO to the specific customer sales order, ensuring all costs are tracked at order level. Do not change this value.

  • Select the Item, in the Material Data tab, click on components.

  • The Special Stock should be set for RM027 as E

  • Go to Account Assignment tab: Sales Order information should be determined.

  • In the Confirmation Tab, select Inbound Delivery under the Confirmation Control.
  • Save the Purchase Order and note down the number.

Step 5: Post Goods Issue to Subcontractor

  • Go to the App Subcontracting Cockpit (F2948) and enter the Purchase Order and click Go, the components details should be displayed.

  • Select the components and click Expand all

  • Then select all the components and click Create Delivery.

  • Ensure all the details are determined, For example for RM027, the Special indicator is E, Sales Order and Its Item number should be displayed

  • Then click Create Delivery. An outbound delivery document should be created, note down the number.
  • Login as Inventory Manager and check the Stock in the App: Display Stock Overview(MMBE) before posting goods issue.
  • Enter the material name SG035, Plant: 1010, Storage location as 101B and click execute

  • Enter Material as RM027, Plant 1010 and Storage loc as 101C and execute

  • Click Detailed display, the stock associated to respective Sales Order should be displayed.

  • Login as Shipping Specialist, go theApp: Change Outbound Delivery(VL02N).
  • Enter the outbound delivery document, which was created in the previous step, go to picking tab and complete the picking process and save the document.
  • Click post goods issue
  • Go to The Document flow of the Outbound Delivery and click on the material document which was created, check the below:
  • For RM027: The Special stock indicator should be E at the Producer and F once moved under Subcontractor’s Stock

  •  Important: No financial/accounting document is generated at Goods Issue, this is a stock-to-stock transfer only. No consumption has occurred yet. The FI/accounting document is generated only at Goods Receipt (Step 8).
  • Financial Document is not generated from outbound delivery, because it's only transfer the material from E stock to Subcontracting stock, no consumption has occurred yet.

Step 6 :Verify Stock at Subcontractor

  • Login as Inventory Manager.
  • You can as well check the stocks of each material in the App: Display stock overview(MMBE). And see the stock details, how many are with subcontracting vendor and how many with Producer. For example:

 

Step 7: Receive Inbound Delivery from Subcontractor

  • Login as Receiving Specialist and Go to App: Change Inbound Delivery(VL32N) and enter the Inbound Delivery number and press ENTER
  • Select the Item and go to the tab: Stock Placement, Enter the Putaway Qty and click Save.

Step 8: Post Goods Receipt

 Note: The Goods Receipt is the trigger for the financial posting. The finished product SG035 is received into Sales Order Stock (E) and the subcontracting fee + material costs are posted against the customer sales order.

  • Go to the App: Post Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery(F2502) and enter the Inbound delivery number which was created in the previous steps.
  • Check the Special Stock type as E and Sales Order and its item number information are predetermined.

  • Select the Item and check the details. Check the component details are reflected rightly with respective special stock type for the component Material RM027

  • Post the Goods Receipt and note down the material document.

Step 9: Verify Material Document and Journal Entry

Doc Info tab → FI Documents → Journal Entry

  • Check the Material Document, following details should be available and Accounting documents should be generated.

  • Click Doc Info tab and click FI Documents

  • Check the Journal Entry:

Step 10: Create Supplier Invoice

  • Note: this is optional because we are focusing on the logistic process

Step 11: Final Stock Verification

  • Check the Stock in theApp: Display Stock Overview(MMBE)

Summary:

Scope item 7DW closes a critical gap in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by enabling full MTO subcontracting with Sales Order Stock (E). Make-to-Order manufacturers can now outsource operations while maintaining complete sales order integrity, preventing cross-consumption, and ensuring accurate order-level cost tracking.

Related Resources:

Assembly Subcontracting with Project Stock (Q) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Scope Item 6GD

Restrictions

entry.title: Assembly Subcontracting with Project Stock (Q) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Scope Item 6GD
entry.content:
Business Case: 

Scooter Manufacturer: Body Panel Painting 

ABC Scooter Company is executing a Government Fleet Project. The Body Panel is manufactured specifically for the project (Project Stock Q), while Primer and Paint are standard consumables issued from Regular Plant Stock. The subcontractor paints the body panel and returns the finished Painted Body Panel back into Project Stock (Q). 

By using Project Stock (Q) in S/4HANA Public Cloud, all materials and subcontracting costs are tied to the specific WBS element, ensuring strict project-level control, accurate budget tracking, and full traceability, critical for delivering complex projects on time and within budget. 

What changed in 2608:

  • The Subcontracting PO can now contain BOM with Components both from Regular inventory stock and Special stock (Project Stock: Q) in the same order.
  •  No stock type conversion required: Q transfers directly to R at the vendor.
  • Full ETO support: all costs tracked to the WBS element throughout GI, GR, and FI.
  • No financial document at Goods Issue: the FI/accounting posting occurs only at Goods Receipt.

For scope item 6GD, the stock type transitions are:

  • Project Stock component (e.g. RM0614): Q (at producer)  →  R – Subcontractor Project Stock (at vendor)  →  Consumed on GR
  • Regular Plant Stock component (e.g. RM0613): Blank ( ) (at producer)  →  O at vendor  →  Consumed on GR
  • Finished product (e.g. SG0612): Received back into Project Stock (Q) on GR
  • No financial or accounting document is generated when components are issued to the subcontractor (Goods Issue). This is a stock-to-stock transfer only — no consumption has occurred yet. The financial posting (cost to WBS element) is triggered only at Goods Receipt.

How to Set-up: 

Example Master Data: SG0612(main material), Components: RM0613 (regular or Plant Stock) and RM0614(Project Stock) 

  • In the material Master of RM0614: Go to MRP4 and set the individual/Collective requirement as 1(Individual) 
  • RM0613: Go to MRP4 and set the Individual/Collective Requirement as 2 (Collective) this allows the component to be planned pooled from plant stock.
  •  Note: The Individual/Collective Requirement setting on MRP4 is the most commonly missed configuration. RM0614 must be set to 1 (Individual) so MRP plans it per WBS element, not pooled. Without this, the project stock link will not be established correctly.

If the stock is insufficient, then Produce or Procure the components RM0613 and RM0614 according to your scenario. 

Step-by-Step Process Guide 

Step 1: Create Project Demand

  • Create a demand in the App: Manage Project Demand(F4562) by entering details as shown in the screenshot. 

Step 2: Run MRP

  • Either schedule MRP Run in the background or Run it as shown below: 
  • Go to the App: Monitor Material Coverage - Net and Individual Segments (Version 2)(F2101A)
  • Enter the Shortage definition as MRP Standard, Material as SG0612 (For which the demand is created in the above step) and then click Go. The details are listed. 

  • Select the demand and click Start MRP Run. 

  • After the MRP run, click Manage Materials. 

  • A Purchase Requisition should be created. 

Step 3: Create Subcontracting Purchase Order

  • Go to the App: Process Purchase Requisitions(F1048A).
  • Enter the Purchase Requisition or filter based on your criteria, select the Purchase Requisitions and assign the supplier, supplier should be determined based on the Purchase InfoRecords.
  • Then Click Create Purchase Order, The Purchase Order Simulation should be displayed.
  • Account Assignment = Q (Project/WBS) and Item Category = L (Subcontracting) must be pre-determined in the PO item table. Do not change these values.
  • In the Item Table, ensure that the account Assignment(Q) and Item Category = L (Subcontracting) pre-determined. Select the Item, in the Material Data tab, click on components. 

  • The Special Stock field must be set to Q for RM0614 whereas for RM0613 it should be blank 

  • Go to Account Assignment tab: WBS information should be determined. 

  • In the Confirmation Tab, select Inbound Delivery under the Confirmation Control. 
  • Save the Purchase Order and note down the number. 

Step 4: Post Goods Issue to Subcontractor

  • Go to the App:Subcontracting Cockpit (F2948) and enter the Purchase Order and click Go, the components details should be displayed. 

  • Select the components and click Expand all 

  • Then select all the components and click Create Delivery. 

  • Ensure all the details are determined, For example for RM0614, the Special indicator is Q, WBS information is as same as for which the demand was generated.  

  • Then click Create Delivery. An outbound delivery document should be created, note down the number. 
  • Login as Inventory Manager and check the Stock in the App: Analyze Stock in Date Range(F6185) before posting goods issue. 

  • Login as Shipping Specialist and go to theApp: Change Outbound Delivery(VL02N).
  • Enter the outbound delivery document, which was created in the previous step, go to picking tab and complete the picking process and save the document. 
  • Click Post Goods Issue 
  • Go to The Document flow of the Outbound Delivery and click on the material document which was created, check the below: for RM0613, once the stock moves to Subcontractor stock, it should be denoted with Special Stock type O (Subcontractor Stock). 

  • Whereas for RM0614: The Special stock indicator should be Q at the Producer and R once moved under subcontractor’s stock 

  •  Important: No financial/accounting document is generated at Goods Issue this is a stock-to-stock transfer only. No consumption has occurred yet. The FI/accounting document is generated only at Goods Receipt (Step 7) 

Step 5: Verify Stock at Subcontractor

  • Login as Inventory Manager and go to the App: Analyze Stock in Date Range(F6185) and check the stock details: 
  • You can as well check the stocks of each material in the App: Display stock overview(MMBE). And see the stock details, how many are with subcontracting vendor and how many with Producer. For example: 

Step 6 : Receive Inbound Delivery from Subcontractor

  • Login as Receiving Specialist and go to App: Change Inbound Delivery(VL32N) and enter the Inbound Delivery number and press ENTER
  • Select the Item and go to the tab: Stock Placement, Enter the Putaway Qty and click Save. 

Step 7: Post Goods Receipt

  • Note: The Goods Receipt is the trigger for the financial posting. The finished product SG0612 is received into Project Stock (Q) and the subcontracting fee + material costs are posted to the WBS element.
  • Go to the App:Post Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery(F2502) and enter the Inbound delivery number which was created in the previous steps. 
  • Check the Special Stock type as Q and WBS information are predetermined. 

  • Select the Item and check the details. 
  • Check the component details are reflected rightly with respective special stock type for the component Material RM0613 and RM0614. 

  • Post the Goods Receipt and note down the material document. 

Step 8: Verify Material Document and Journal Entry

Doc Info tab → FI Documents → Journal Entry

  • Check the Material Document, following details should be available and Accounting documents should be generated. 

  • Click Doc Info tab and click FI Documents 

  • Check the Journal Entry: 

 Step 9: Create Supplier Invoice

  • Note: this is optional because we are focusing on the logistic process

Step 10 : Final Stock Verification

  • Check the Stock in the App: Analyze Stock in Date Range(F6185)

Summary:

Scope item 6GD closes a critical gap in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by enabling full ETO subcontracting with Project Stock (Q). Manufacturers can now outsource operations while maintaining strict WBS-level cost control, complete material traceability, and automated financial posting all within a single integrated process.

Related Resources:

Assembly Subcontracting with Sales Order Stock (E) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: 7DW

Restrictions

entry.title: AI Powered i-TS (Intelligent Test Scoper) for Post Upgrade Test of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Releases
entry.content:
Introduction

Every SAP S/4HANA Cloud release brings exciting innovations and continuous business improvements. While these enhancements deliver greater value to customers, they also introduce the need to validate if already available business processes continue to function seamlessly post upgrade to every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Continuous Feature delivery (CFD)

Testing therefore becomes an essential part of every upgrade cycle. However, executing the complete Test Suite for regression test of every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release and CFD is time and resource intensive. The key challenge is no longer whether to test, but what to test?.

This challenge inspired the development of the Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) (See Figure-1) an application designed to recommend the optimal Post Upgrade Test (PUT) scope for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers.

Figure-1

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud CE 2608 release, we introduce AI-powered i-TS that delivers more efficient yet effective scope for Post Upgrade Tests, thereby further reducing the TCO for out esteemed Customers.

The Journey of Intelligent Test Scoper

Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) are a key offering for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers to validate software upgrades before consumption in productive environments.

The objective is simple:

  • Maintain software quality
  • Reduce effort and upgrade validation time
  • Sustain confidence in Release quality

Traditionally, PUT testing relied on executing even the entire suite of standard scripts delivered (via SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool) . While this approach maximized confidence, it also resulted in significant execution effort and longer upgrade cycles, not to mention higher TCO.

To address this challenge, Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) was introduced to automatically derive an optimized PUT scope based on SAP Standard code changes delivered in every Cloud Release or Continuous Feature Delivery (CFD).

How i-TS Determines the Optimized Scope

The working principle of i-TS can be understood using the following Venn diagram.

Consider the rectangle representing all development objects within an application area.

  • The blue circle represents the objects touched by SAP Standard PUT automates.
  • The yellow circle represents the objects changed in a Cloud Release or CFD.

The overlapping region identifies the automation processes affected by code changes. These processes form the optimized regression scope proposed by Intelligent Test Scoper.

This approach has successfully enabled customers to execute only those automated tests that are technically impacted by a release, reducing unnecessary regression execution.

Why Go Beyond Code Change Analysis?

While code-change analysis marked a major step in the direction of intelligent regression testing, our engineering team continued asking an important question:

Can Artificial Intelligence further reduce the scope proposal while maintaining the same quality standards?

The answer lay in recognizing that code changes alone do not always represent the complete business impact. Several additional factors influence whether a business process should be executed after an upgrade:

  • How frequently customers use the application
  • The overall technical complexity of a test process
  • The relationship between changed and executed development objects
  • Historical regression behaviour observed across previous releases

By learning from these signals collectively, AI has the potential to identify patterns that traditional rule-based approaches cannot.

Building the AI Foundation

As with every successful AI initiative, the journey starts with data. Our objective was not to replace the existing code-change logic, but to enhance it with additional intelligence derived from historical observations.

The AI model was trained using multiple dimensions of information, including:

  • Code Change Information: The optimized scope proposed by the existing i-TS solution based on SAP Standard code changes.
  • Customer Relevancy: Application usage information indicating how frequently customers (cumulatively) execute individual business processes.
  • Process Complexity: The number of ABAP development objects touched by each automated test process across different object types.
  • Change-to-Touch Ratio: The relationship between changed development objects and the total objects touched by an automation process.

Together, these parameters provide a richer representation of the business impact associated with every automated test process.

Training the AI Model

Building confidence in AI recommendations required extensive validation; Instead of relying only on historical accuracy, we simulated real upgrade scenarios by training the model on previous releases and predicting scope for future releases.

 

Training ReleasePrediction Release
CE 2502CE 2408
CE 2502 + CE 2408CE 2508
CE 2502 + CE 2408 + CE 2508CE 2602

This approach closely mirrors the way customers consume SAP S/4HANA Cloud upgrades in production. For every prediction cycle, the AI-generated scope was carefully validated against actual PUT outcomes to ensure there was no quality leakage while measuring optimization gains.

This iterative learning process enabled continuous refinement of the prediction model before introducing it into productive scope recommendations.

The Results

The outcome exceeded our expectations. Earlier releases of Intelligent Test Scoper relied exclusively on SAP Standard code-change analysis and consistently delivered approximately 10% optimization in comparison to the full suite of standard automates.

With of AI-powered scope optimization, i-TS is now able to achieve nearly double the optimization efficiency while continuing to maintain the same quality standards.

Rather than replacing engineering expertise, AI complements the existing code-change analysis by learning from historical release behaviour and customer usage patterns to make more informed scope recommendations.

What's New in CE 2608

Beginning with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud CE 2608 release, Intelligent Test Scoper proposes optimized regression scope using Artificial Intelligence.

The AI-powered scope recommendation combines:

  • SAP Standard code changes
  • Process complexity
  • Change-to-touch ratio
  • Historical learning from previous cloud releases

The result is a smarter regression scope that enables customers to:

  • Execute fewer regression tests without compromising quality
  • Reduce upgrade validation effort
  • Shorten regression execution time
  • Lower testing costs
  • Increase confidence during every SAP S/4HANA Cloud upgrade

Looking Ahead

AI is transforming the way software quality engineering is approached, and Intelligent Test Scoper is an important milestone in that journey.

The introduction of AI-powered scope optimization in CE 2608 demonstrates how historical engineering knowledge, customer usage insights, and machine learning can work together to deliver more intelligent testing recommendations.

As we continue evolving i-TS, our focus remains unchanged: helping SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers perform faster, smarter, and more reliable upgrade testing while maximizing efficiency and maintaining the highest standards of software quality.

We are excited about this journey and look forward to continuously enhance the capabilities to Intelligent Test Scoper in future releases. Refer following Help Portal Documentation to learn more about i-TS (Intelligent test Scoper)

entry.title: Talk to Your Localized Reports: How Joule Runs A/P, A/R, and Stock Reports in Native Language
entry.content:
Running a country-specific aging report used to mean remembering the transaction code, the exact parameter names, and the local compliance rules that shape every field. Now you can just ask.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces AI-assisted localized business data management — a new capability that lets Joule, SAP's generative AI digital assistant, run classic localized reports through natural-language prompts while respecting every country- and region-specific requirement.

Scope Note

This blog post covers SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and the new AI Case Manage Business Data Using Localized Classic Applications. Activation requires the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG and the corresponding business catalog per report. Three localized reports are currently in scope: the A/P Aging Report, the A/R Aging Report, and the Stock Overview Report. The reports were originally built for specific countries, but any customer can adopt them wherever they see a fit — simply copy the delivered business catalog into your own business role and assign it to the target users. Other editions (on-premise, Cloud Private Edition) and other localized reports are not covered here.

Main Insight

Localized reports are where compliance meets complexity. Every country encodes its own rules for aging buckets, currency handling, cleared-item treatment, and inventory posting — and each rule shows up as another parameter on a classic report screen.

The result: teams spend more time configuring the report than reading it. New users take weeks to get productive. And every parameter mis-entry is a potential compliance gap.

AI-Assisted Localized Business Data Management flips that. Joule sits on top of the same classic reports your finance and supply-chain teams already use — but it understands the country-specific context. You describe what you need in plain English. Joule fills the parameters, respects the localization rules, and runs the report.

You keep the compliance-certified backend. You lose the friction.

Three Reports, One Conversational Interface

Three classic localized reports ship with the initial release. Each requires its own business catalog assigned to the user.

1. Accounts Payable (A/P) Aging Report

  • Report ID: RFIDCN_AP_AGING
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by company code, supplier, and key date · select item types · configure due-date buckets and currency · analyze amounts in transaction or reporting currency
  • Example prompt: "Run the A/P Aging Report for specific suppliers or supplier ranges within a company."

2. Accounts Receivable (A/R) Aging Report

  • Report ID: RFIDCN_AR_AGING
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAR_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by company code, customer, and key date · include or exclude cleared and special G/L items · define due-date buckets and reporting currency
  • Example prompt: "Run the A/R Aging Report for customers S13100251 to S13100253 in company 1310."

3. Stock Overview Report

  • Report ID: J_3RMOBVEDH
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_MOBVED_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by plant and product number · select a posting date or date range · filter by storage location and batch · review inventory status in context
  • Example prompt: "Run the Stock Overview Report for product tg-d010_ua in plant 6510 with posting date today."

Same reports. Same compliance guarantees. New way to reach them.

Not restricted to the original country. These reports ship as SAP-delivered business catalogs, but you don't have to run them only in the country they were originally designed for. Copy the delivered catalog into a custom business role and assign the role to any user or business unit that needs the same reporting logic. For step-by-step guidance, see Copying and Editing Business Roles — SAP Help Portal.

Activation in 6 Steps

The capability is off by default. Enable it with the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG:

  1. Open your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition configuration environment with an administrator user that has the required authorization.
  2. Search for and open the configuration activity Activate Features.
  3. In the feature list, search for the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG.
  4. Review the feature details to confirm you are activating the AI-assisted localized business data management capability.
  5. Choose the activation option and confirm when prompted.
  6. Wait until the system confirms successful activation, then assign the required business catalog(s) to your users.

Once activated, users can invoke Joule from any Joule-enabled entry point in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and prompt the localized reports directly.

Practical Takeaway

Start with one report and one country. Pick the localized report your team runs most often — A/P Aging for a China entity is a good candidate — and follow this path:

  • Verify your system runs SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Joule enabled
  • Activate feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG in a test tenant
  • Assign the matching business catalog (e.g. SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC) to a pilot user
  • Ask Joule to run the report using one of the example prompts above
  • Compare Joule's parameter selections to what your team would have entered manually
  • Roll out to the full team once you confirm the compliance rules and outputs match

You'll know it's working when your finance team stops asking "which parameter goes where?" and starts asking "can I get the same report for last month?" — because now they can.

Summary

Localized reports carry the weight of country-specific compliance, and that complexity usually lives in the report screen. AI-Assisted Localized Business Data Management moves the complexity into Joule — users describe what they need in natural language, and the classic report runs with the correct localized parameters. Three reports ship first (A/P Aging, A/R Aging, Stock Overview), with more expected as the capability expands. Because the reports are delivered as SAP business catalogs, you can copy them into your own business roles and reuse them wherever the reporting logic fits your process — not only in the country they were originally built for.

Key Takeaways

  • Compliance stays in the backend. Joule doesn't reinterpret local rules — it drives the existing compliance-certified classic reports, so audit and regulatory guarantees remain intact.
  • Activation is a single feature toggle. GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG in the Activate Features configuration activity turns the capability on. No custom development required.
  • Business catalog assignment is per report. Each of the three initial reports has its own catalog (SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC, _IDCNAR_PC, _MOBVED_PC) — assign only what each user needs.
  • Productivity gain is immediate. New users skip the parameter-memorization phase. Experienced users skip the parameter-entry step. Both groups reach the same compliant result faster.
  • Scope is intentionally narrow at launch. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition only, three reports only. Plan pilots around what's shipped today, and monitor SAP Help Portal for the expanding scope.
  • Reuse beyond the original country. Copy the delivered business catalogs into custom business roles and assign them wherever the reporting logic fits — the reports aren't locked to the country they were originally designed for.

More Information

  • AI-Assisted Localized Business Data Management — SAP Help Portal
  • Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition — SAP Help Portal
  • Activating Business AI and Assigning Users — SAP Help Portal
  • Copying and Editing Business Roles — SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in Georgia Using Localization as a Self-Service (CLT)
entry.content:
Understanding Customer Local Versions

SAP delivers standard local versions for many countries. These include country-specific configuration, legal requirements, tax settings, reporting capabilities, and SAP Best Practices maintained by SAP.

However, not every country has a standard SAP localization.

For these countries, SAP provides Localization as a Self-Service (), allowing customers to create a Customer Local Version using the Configuration Localization Tool (CLT).

It is important to understand that this approach differs fundamentally from implementing an SAP-delivered country version.

 

SAP-Delivered Local Version vs. Customer Local Version

SAP-Delivered Local Version Customer Local Version (CLT)
Maintained by SAPMaintained by customer or implementation partner
Country-specific Best PracticesDerived initially from Germany Best Practices
SAP legal updates deliveredCustomer responsible for future legal changes
Standard localization contentCustomer configures localization requirements
SAP-supported legal contentCustomer validates local compliance

This distinction defines the implementation approach from the very beginning.

What Does Configuration Localization Tool (CLT) Actually Do?

CLT creates the technical foundation for a Customer Local Version by deriving the initial configuration from the SAP-delivered local version for Germany, which is currently the only supported source country.

CLT helps create country-specific configuration objects that can then be adapted using released configuration activities and extensibility.

It does not automatically deliver:

  • Georgian VAT configuration
  • Georgian statutory reports
  • Georgian legal forms
  • Georgian payment formats
  • Georgian tax rules
  • Country-specific legal updates

These elements remain the responsibility of the customer and implementation partner.

Why Germany Is the Source Country

A common question during project planning is:

Which source country should we choose?

According to SAP Note 3107866, the answer is straightforward:

Germany is currently the only supported source country for CLT.

This means that every Customer Local Version initially derives its configuration from Germany.

The implementation team must then analyze the legal and business requirements of the target country and adapt the configuration where supported by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Understanding this principle early helps avoid unrealistic expectations regarding the amount of localization delivered automatically.

Project Preparation Is the Most Critical Success Factor

SAP places significant emphasis on preparation before activating CLT.

Once a Customer Local Version has been activated, it cannot be rolled back, deleted, purged, or concealed. This makes early planning essential.

Before creating a Customer Local Version, implementation teams should conduct a comprehensive localization readiness assessment covering areas such as:

  • Corporate legal requirements
  • Financial accounting
  • VAT and indirect tax processes
  • Banking and payment methods
  • Statutory reporting
  • Document layouts and forms
  • Integration requirements
  • Master data requirements
  • Asset Accounting
  • Country-specific business processes

The earlier localization gaps are identified, the lower the overall project risk.

Recommended Implementation Approach

A practical implementation approach for a country such as Georgia can be structured into five phases:

1. Localization Readiness Assessment

  • Analyse legal and regulatory requirements.
  • Identify gaps between Germany Best Practices and Georgian requirements.
  • Define localization ownership and governance.

2. Customer Local Version Creation

  • Create the Customer Local Version using CLT.
  • Validate organizational structure.
  • Review generated configuration.

3. Configuration

  • Adapt available SSCUI settings.
  • Configure finance and tax settings where supported.
  • Maintain master data and organizational structures.
  • Configure forms and document layouts.
  • Develop approved extensions where necessary while maintaining a Clean Core.

4. Testing

  • Validate localization scenarios.
  • Execute end-to-end finance processes.
  • Verify tax calculations.
  • Test integrations.
  • Confirm statutory reporting requirements.

5. Go-Live and Continuous Improvement

  • Execute cutover.
  • Monitor localization processes during Hypercare.
  • Establish a process for maintaining future legal changes.

Working with Parallel Line in CBC

One recommendation in SAP Note 3107866 deserves particular attention.

SAP recommends completing the entire localization configuration in the Parallel Line, including Product Configuration—not only organizational structure and scope items.

Only after all activities have been completed should the configuration be merged into the Main Line.

This approach reduces the risk of major issues that could otherwise require a reset of the Parallel Line.

Another important consideration is that configuration changes related to the ongoing CLT implementation should not be performed directly in the Main Line while localization work is still in progress.

Key Limitations Every Project Team Should Know

Understanding the limitations of CLT is just as important as understanding its capabilities.

Some of the most significant restrictions currently include:

  • CLT is available only for customers using the 3-System Landscape (3SL).
  • Germany is currently the only supported source country.
  • Demo Data cannot be used together with CLT.
  • The Starter System cannot currently be provisioned with CLT.
  • Future legal changes for Customer Local Versions are not delivered by SAP and must be maintained by the customer or implementation partner.
  • Once activated, a Customer Local Version cannot be removed or rolled back.
  • Some localization scenarios remain unsupported and should be verified against the latest SAP documentation before project planning.

These limitations should be incorporated into the project's risk assessment and governance model from the outset.

Maintaining a Clean Core

Localization projects often create pressure for custom developments.

However, SAP's recommended approach is to keep the ERP core as standard as possible.

Implementation teams should first evaluate:

  • Released configuration activities (SSCUIs)
  • In-App Extensibility
  • Key User Extensibility
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (where appropriate)

Only after exhausting these options should additional development be considered, always within SAP's released extensibility framework.

A Clean Core strategy reduces upgrade risk and simplifies long-term maintenance.

Lessons Learned

Having participated in multiple SAP S/4HANA Cloud transformation programs, several practical lessons consistently emerge:

Localization is not a technical activity—it is a business transformation activity.

Many localization challenges originate from legal, financial, or operational requirements rather than system functionality.

Early involvement of local finance experts is essential.

Tax specialists, finance users, and local compliance experts should participate from the beginning of the project.

Do not assume that a Customer Local Version is equivalent to a standard SAP country version.

CLT provides the framework, but successful localization depends on careful planning, configuration, testing, and governance.

Treat localization as a continuous process.

Unlike SAP-delivered local versions, Customer Local Versions require organizations to monitor legal changes and maintain compliance over time.

Conclusion

Localization as a Self-Service significantly expands the reach of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by enabling implementations in countries where SAP does not provide a standard local version.

For organizations operating in Georgia, CLT provides the foundation for creating a Customer Local Version, but it should not be viewed as a complete localization solution. Instead, it provides a structured framework that implementation teams can build upon using released configuration, extensibility, and robust project governance.

Successful implementations depend on thorough preparation, early localization analysis, disciplined use of SAP Central Business Configuration, and a commitment to maintaining a Clean Core. By understanding both the capabilities and limitations of CLT, project teams can deliver compliant, scalable, and upgrade-ready SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solutions for countries beyond SAP's standard localization portfolio. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/60a09f68f2444ceca31dcac2e7017945/f5ce21aa2d894f9d92fc17557080b86c.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions of AI for Your Implementation
entry.content:
This is the first blog post in a series introducing the topic of AI in SAP Activate. Follow this blog as we will update it with links to other posts as they are released.

Last update: July 15th 2026, with links to additional related blog posts at the end of the article.

In this blog post, I want to share how SAP Activate is evolving with SAP Business AI and what is already available to you today to leverage in your implementation projects. As SAP expands the availability of AI capabilities and more assistants and agents become available for our customers, SAP Activate is keeping pace to ensure you have the guidance you need to adopt, extend, and apply these capabilities effectively during your journey to the Autonomous Suite.

When we think about Business AI in the context of SAP Activate, we organize it into three interconnected dimensions. Each dimension addresses a different aspect of how AI applies in the implementation lifecycle. In the rest of this post, I will walk you through each of these dimensions, explain what is available today, and highlight how you can start benefiting from these capabilities right away.

Three Dimensions of Business AI in SAP Activate

Before I get into the details, let me introduce the framework. The three dimensions are:

  1. Adopting Business AI capabilities and SAP Joule by SAP customers during their implementation and beyond.
  2. Extending SAP solutions with AI capabilities and agents built on the SAP Business AI Platform.
  3. Leveraging AI capabilities for implementation activities.

These dimensions are interconnected but address different audiences and project stages. Let me expand on each one.

1. Adopting SAP Business AI and SAP Joule

The first dimension is about guiding you, the customer, to activate, set up, and adopt SAP Joule and SAP Business AI capabilities as part of your implementation. In other words, this is about integrating AI into how your organization runs its business processes in the SAP Autonomous Suite.

Think of it this way: when your project team starts scoping the implementation and later runs fit-to-standard workshops, they should be considering not only the standard business processes but also the AI capabilities that come with them. Which processes benefit from AI-powered automation or recommendations? Where does SAP Joule provide value to your end users in their day-to-day work? What agents and assistants would enrich the business processes in our organization? These are the questions SAP Activate helps you answer.

Already in 2025, SAP Activate was enhanced with guidance to help customers activate, set up, and adopt SAP Joule and available SAP Business AI capabilities (see the Business AI catalog on SAP Discovery Center). The guidance in SAP Activate includes self-enablement tasks, guidance for running fit-to-standard workshops with AI considerations, and procedures for setting up the necessary integrations. As an example, the information for the setup of SAP Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is available here (the tasks are tagged with Joule tag).

How do you find this content? You can select the SAP Business AI and SAP Joule tags in the road map viewer in SAP for Me to identify the relevant tasks for your implementation scenario. The flow is straightforward: identify the available SAP Business AI capabilities and SAP Joule features, assess and validate them during fit-to-standard, activate and set up during Realize, and enable your business users to adopt the new capability.

Example of a task for the initial setup of Joule in SAP Cloud ERP Private

We plan to continue expanding this coverage throughout 2026, with increased focus on the new AI capabilities and agents as they become available to customers, and to incorporate steps to consider and embed these capabilities into the scope of your implementation.

Dimension 2: Extending with SAP Business AI Platform Services

The second dimension is for organizations that need to go beyond the pre-built AI capabilities. It supports customers in leveraging the SAP Business AI Platform to build custom AI solutions and agents that extend standard SAP application capabilities in line with the clean core strategy.

This is an important distinction: the first dimension is about adopting what SAP delivers out of the box. The second dimension is about building your own AI-powered extensions on the platform when your business requires something unique. The key principle here is that these extensions follow the clean core approach, ensuring your solution remains upgradeable and maintainable.

SAP Activate for SAP Business Technology Platform already covers various AI-related topics, including guidance for application development on SAP BTP and implementation guidance for AI capabilities. The use of platform services to extend solution guidance is also embedded in the implementation roadmaps for our ERP solutions in the Extensibility work stream, for both private and public cloud solutions. You can access it here.

The flow in your project follows the extensibility work stream: identify and confirm extensibility requirements during fit-to-standard workshops (or earlier if the requirements are already known), leverage SAP Business AI Platform services to extend the standard solution and test during the Realize phase, and finally enable your business users to adopt the new capability.

We plan to continue evolving this implementation guidance as new platform capabilities and services become available, including the new agents and assistants announced at SAPPHIRE 2026.

Dimension 3: Leveraging AI Capabilities for Implementation

The third dimension is where I expect many of you reading this post will find the most immediate value. This is about applying AI capabilities throughout your implementation project to accelerate requirements gathering, design, development, testing, and knowledge transfer across the project lifecycle.

This dimension covers a broad range of AI capabilities like Joule on road map viewer, SAP Joule for Consultants, and embedded AI capabilities in the integrated toolchain, including SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and WalkMe.

AI-Assisted Tasks

Earlier in June, SAP Activate released the first wave of AI-assisted implementation tasks. This initial release included 9 updated SAP Activate tasks, 13 new tested prompts, and updated accelerators, focused on SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

These AI-assisted tasks are designed to support your project team in leveraging AI capabilities during implementation. Rather than focusing on information retrieval, we focused on high-impact tasks and deliverables where AI can make a real difference. SAP Activate delivers tested prompts and example outputs, and in some cases, updated accelerators.

The initial focus is on Joule for Consultants, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP Test Automation Tool through all project stages, covering seven front-runner topics:

  1. Self-enablement on Joule for Consultants – helping your team get started with the capability.
  2. Project execution and artifacts preparation – accelerating the creation of project management deliverables.
  3. Preparation for fit-to-standard workshops – using AI to help consultants prepare effectively for these critical sessions.
  4. Generating requirements from fit-to-standard workshop artifacts – processing workshop results into requirements and user stories in SAP Cloud ALM with assistance from Joule for Consultants.
  5. Generating design documents for extensions – using AI to draft extension design documentation.
  6. Preparing for data loads  – leveraging AI to help with data migration strategy and approach.
  7. Testing – preparing testing strategy and approach, as well as automating preparation of test scripts, determination of test scope, and management of testing.

An AI-assisted task includes two key elements: an AI-assisted procedure section in the task description that explains how to use the AI capability, and a link to the prompt library. The prompt library provides access to the prompt text, example output, and additional details for prompting the AI tool. We built a centralized prompt library to maintain the integrity of prompts linked to SAP Activate and to ensure all users can access them, even if they do not yet have direct access to the AI capability where a dedicated prompt library may exist.

You can find these tasks by filtering for the AI-Assisted Procedure tag in the SAP Activate roadmap viewer or SAP Cloud ALM when accessing the Task Management app. The SAP Activate team will continue to expand the coverage with AI-assisted tasks in the future. Let us know your feedback on the current tasks and your suggestions for areas to enhance.

Joule in SAP Activate Road Map Viewer on SAP for Me

I also want to highlight a capability that has been available since June 2025 and continues to bring great value to our users. SAP Joule is embedded directly in the SAP Activate road map viewer on SAP for Me. This is a conversational AI assistant that enables you to surface the guidance you need through natural-language questions, eliminating the need to manually navigate the roadmap structures.

You can ask Joule your SAP Activate-related questions, carry out a conversation, and copy the answers to share with your partners or colleagues. For example, you might ask about the sequence of activities for data migration, or about which accelerators are available for a specific topic. Joule will provide a clear response based on the SAP Activate content.

To try it, navigate to the SAP Activate road map viewer and click on the Joule icon in the top right corner. You can read more about this capability in the blog post from Chandan: Say Hi to Joule in SAP Activate road map viewer.

Outlook

Looking ahead, we will continue enriching SAP Activate with AI guidance for implementation experts – project managers, consultants, developers, and architects – on how to use available AI capabilities during projects.

In the mid-to-long term, we will evolve SAP Activate as new agents and assistants become available in SAP Cloud ALM, aligned with the agentic AI transformation platform vision shared at SAPPHIRE 2026. We anticipate significant evolution as new agents are built and integrated into the offerings through 2026.

I encourage you to explore the AI-assisted tasks in the road map viewer, try Joule on SAP for Me, and share your experience. Please use the comments to this blog post to continue the conversation or reach out to us at sapactivatemethodology@sap.com with your feedback and suggestions. We are excited to hear how you are leveraging these capabilities in your projects.

Other blogs in this series

To deep-dive into AI-assisted tasks and capabilities, access the blogs linked below (we will add the links as the blogs get published).

  • Introducing the SAP Activate Prompt Library: Curated AI Prompts for Your Implementation Project
  • AI-Assisted Extensibility Planning & Design in SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Work Smarter with AI-Assisted Tasks in the SAP Activate Project Management Workstream

Useful Resources

  • SAP Activate road map viewer
  • Introducing Joule
  • Setting Up Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscapes
  • Introducing SAP Joule for Consultants
  • Implementing Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscape

entry.title: Manage Sales Documents App: Introducing Implicit Filters
entry.content:
Background

In this blog, I'm going to write about some implicit filters in the app Manage Sales Documents (MSD).

The Filters

Filter Data via Custom Fields

This is a long-standing impediment in VA05. Now it is tackled partially in MSD.

I assume that there are already custom fields in your systems, and you have been using them in the various sales documents. The expectation is to be able to use them as filters to query data in the list report.

The first thing to do is to Enable the fields for the service of MSD in Custom Fields app.

There are two fields in this example: one for the header, the other for the item.

Custom Field for Sales Document Header

Preparation

Create a field for header:

Enable usage for the service of MSD

Custom Field for Sales Document Item

Preparation

Create a field for item

Enable usage for the service of MSD

Consumption in MSD

Use adapt filters to expose the two filter fields.

The header filter works on all three tabs.

The item filter works on items & schedule lines tabs.

Since it has no effect on Sales Documents tab, as a result, this tab loads all data, an information will be displayed to explain the behavior: Filter "Extended Item Date" is not relevant for tab "Sales Documents". Setting this filter has no effect on the results.'

Sales Document (One-Time Customer) Filter

When using a one-time customer to create sales orders, it is mandatory to change the contact info such as address, name, phone number, email etc. However, the customer number stays the same, thus, it is not possible to precisely filter the documents using just the number. So, we built this filter that can query sales documents via the contact info of a one-time customer.

Open the value help

Filter data via Telephone

Filter data via Name

entry.title: Component Colour Management with AVC and Material Variants in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) in SAP S/4HANA is a tool for managing complex configurable products. It's often positioned as the make-to-order or engineer-to-order sales configurator for S/4HANA, but it can also support different lightweight use cases. In this blog post, I will describe one manufacturing and logistics related use case: component colour management.

Use Case Introduction

Let’s introduce the use case with the classic bike example. Bikes are sold in many different colours, and the selected colour must be transferred from sales order item through BOM to relevant components. These components need the colour information so they can either be purchased in the correct colour or painted in-house. Not all components, like bike tires, are colour-relevant. Only colour-relevant components should be affected by the selected colour. The key challenge is logistics: different colours must be separated in inventory and in downstream logistics processes.

To put it as a business requirement:

  • Colour is only selected in the sales order item.
  • Only colour-relevant components inherit the colour.
  • Inventory must be separated by different colours.

Solution

The core idea is to model colour as an AVC characteristic on the finished product and use BOM class items to select the correct material variant for each colour-relevant component. Material variants are needed because colour must become logistics-relevant. It is possible to transfer the AVC colour characteristic to the generic component, but this has no impact on logistics processes. Alternatively, batch management could be used, but there is no standard automatic way to transfer AVC characteristics to batch classification.

The solution process flow is as follows:

  1. Colour is selected in the sales order item configuration.
  2. During BOM explosion, the BOM class item uses the colour value to determine the matching material variant.
  3. The selected coloured material variant is then used in downstream logistics processes such as procurement, production, inventory management, and picking.

Setup

The example below is based on S/4HANA Public Cloud, but the functionality should be the same in Private Cloud as well.

The first step is to define colour as a characteristic and assign it to an AVC class (type 300). In my example, the colour is defined with RAL codes, so the characteristic is a 4-digit numeric value.

Important note: to enable material variant selection through class items, the class must be material-specific. In my example, RM440 is the material number for the component. This means that a class must be created for each colour-relevant component. For a productive implementation, it probably makes sense to automate this.

Class and characteristic

Material variants must be created for all possible colour variants, with material-specific class assigned and colour value defined. Again, for a productive implementation, automation is recommended. Generic configurable “parent” component (here RM440) is not strictly mandatory, but makes managing the variants easier.

Material variantsExample of variant

To enable material variant selection based on the colour characteristic, a class item must be added to the BOM.

BOM with class item

The finished product must be configurable (KMAT) and have a class assigned with the colour characteristic. It’s not necessary to assign the material-specific class to the finished product, as long as the same colour characteristic is available there. In the example, I used a generic colour class, which also enables selection of multiple colour-relevant components based on one characteristic value in the sales order item.

Finished product with class assignment

Configuration profile must also exist for the finished product.

Finished product configuration profile

Demo

That’s the setup: no AVC dependencies are needed. Below is a short demo video showing how it works in simulation mode.

Conclusion

This setup provides a clean-core-friendly solution for component colour management where colour must be logistics-relevant. The main trade-off is material master complexity: using material variants leads to a high number of materials, making material master maintenance more challenging. For that reason, automation and proper master data management are important for productive use.

entry.title: Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): Enablement of Sales Kits
entry.content:
• Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): Enablement of Sales Kits
  • Overview
  • Capability and Benefit
  • Overview of Differences in Processing Sales Kit Items in Advanced Intercompany Sales
  • More Information/ Additional links
  • SAP Help
  • Scope Items
  • Blogs and FAQs
  • Notes - Restrictions and General Information

Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): Enablement of Sales Kits

Overview

With SAP Cloud ERP 2602 ​Sales kit (Sales BOM) processing can now be enabled in advanced intercompany sales. This feature increase operational efficiency and reduce complexity by using predefined products that consist of several components. This feature reduces manual effort and increases automation by using Monitor Value Chain to automatically post the intercompany purchase document and the intercompany sales order for inventory-controlled goods, and to monitor further and orchestrate the process.

The following graphic illustrates the business process:

Graphic: Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): enablement of sales kits

Capability and Benefit

This capability lets a selling organization offer a sales kit while an affiliated delivering company fulfills it. A kit contains both inventory-controlled items and at least one non-inventory-controlled item; intercompany billing and stock movements apply only to the inventory-controlled components. The process supports multiple kit types already available in sell-from-stock, ranging from main-item level processing to bracket-style kits where sub-items carry pricing, inventory control, and delivery. When the delivering plant’s company code differs from the selling company’s, the advanced intercompany flow is triggered: the customer-facing sales order holds the full kit, while the intercompany PO, SO, and invoices include only the stock-managed sub-items. The outbound delivery shows the full kit, with goods movements on inventory-relevant sub-items, and the Value Chain Monitoring app automatically creates and orchestrates intercompany documents with end-to-end document flow visibility.

This enables multinational sales of complete kits without adding operational complexity, ensuring costs and revenues are correctly posted between affiliates for the inventory-managed components. Predefined multi-component products reduce manual steps and errors, while automation via Value Chain Monitoring minimizes effort and shortens cycle times. Supporting multiple kit types provides flexibility to fit varied pricing and fulfillment patterns, helping standardize cross-company operations and improve transparency. Overall, it boosts revenue opportunities with kits, streamlines intercompany processing, and enhances financial accuracy across delivering and selling entities.

Overview of Differences in Processing Sales Kit Items in Advanced Intercompany Sales

The following table gives you an overview of the most important differences between the available ways of processing for items:

  • Processing on Main Item Level (ERLA)
  • Processing on Subitem Level (LUMF)
  • Processing on Both Main Item and Subitem Level: Pricing on Main Item Level (CPFH)
  • Processing on Both Main Item and Subitem Level: Pricing on Subitem Level (DISH)

Table: Overview of Differences in Processing Sales Kit Items in Advanced Intercompany Sales

For CPFH and DISH the profitability segment is propagated (that is, copied, along with other related account assignment fields) from the main item to the subitems.

This video explains the concept:

 

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

More Information / Additional links

SAP Help

  • Advanced Intercompany Sales

  • Sales Kits in Advanced Intercompany Sales
  • Sales Kits in Sales Documents
  • Monitor Value Chains

Scope Items

Best Practices Explorer (including process flow, test-script and set-up instructions):

  • 5D2 - Advanced Intercompany Sales

Blogs and FAQs

  • Advanced Intercompany Sales & Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer
  • Advanced Intercompany Sales - Insights into Value Chain
  • Advanced Intercompany Sales MTO and Variant Configuration (7DM) in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
  • How Transit Plants work in the Advanced Intercompany Sales
  • FAQ new Advanced Intercompany Processes (IC-Sales and IC-Stock Transfer) Procurement
  • Intercompany integration scenarios FAQ in Cloud ERP, Public Edition

 Notes - Restrictions and General Information

  • 3192584 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Restrictions and General Information for Advanced Intercompany Sales
  • 3192546 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Restrictions and General Information for Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer
  • 3100892 - Group Valuation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • 3093830 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Release Restriction Note for Group Valuation

entry.title: Using Custom Logic in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to Realize Insufficient Stock Quantity at Outbound Deliver
entry.content:
Problem Background

A customer has requested a feature where, due to business management requirements, they want to automatically check whether the stock quantity of materials in outbound delivery line items is sufficient when creating the delivery. Specifically, when an outbound delivery is saved, the system needs to verify if the material quantity in the corresponding sales order line items exceeds the available stock quantity. If stock is insufficient, a Warning message should be displayed indicating the material shortage. Additionally, the customer wants to see the corresponding messages when creating outbound deliveries via API.

Solution

We implemented a custom logic solution using a combination of two BAdIs to meet the requirements.

Analysis Steps

1. Identifying the Appropriate BAdI Enhancement Points

First, we needed to find suitable BAdI enhancement points to implement the stock check functionality. After analysis, we discovered that the enhancement point LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK can execute final checks before the delivery is saved. Therefore, this BAdI can be used to return Warning messages when creating outbound deliveries in the frontend APP if stock quantity is insufficient.

2. Implementing Warning Messages for Frontend APP

We implemented basic stock check logic in BAdI LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK:

    CONSTANTS:
    lc_plant    TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
    lc_vkorg    TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
    lc_vtweg    TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
    lc_spart    TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

    DATA:
    lv_so_qty    TYPE menge_d,
    lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

    IF documentprocessingmode = 'CREATE'.
      LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item) .
      IF ls_item-plant = lc_plant.
      " Get Order Quantity of Sales Order Items
      SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
      FROM i_salesorderitem
      WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
        AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
      INTO @LV_so_qty.
      " Get Stock Quantity
      SELECT SINGLE MATLWRHSSTKQTYINMATLBASEUNIT
      FROM I_STOCKQUANTITYCURRENTVALUE_2( P_DisplayCurrency = 'CNY' )
      WHERE PRODUCT = @LS_item-MATERIAL
      INTO @LV_stock.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        CONTINUE. " No SO item found – skip this delivery item
      ENDIF.
      IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
        message-messagetype = 'W'. " saving of the delivery document will be warned
        message-messagetext = |Warning: The material has insufficient stock quantity|.
        EXIT.
      ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

3. Discovering API Scenario Limitations

However, this Warning message only displays in the frontend APP. Messages cannot be seen when creating deliveries via API. Therefore, we needed to implement an alternative solution.

4. Implementing Message Display for API Scenarios

To make the messages visible in API creation scenarios, we adopted the following approach:

Solution Details:

- Create a custom field that can be seen in the API Response
- Use BAdI LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE, which can modify standard and custom fields at both header and line item levels
- This BAdI is called before the delivery is saved to the database

Implementation Code:

IF documentprocessingmode = 'CREATE'.
  CONSTANTS:
    lc_plant  TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
    lc_vkorg  TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
    lc_vtweg  TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
    lc_spart  TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

  DATA:
    lv_so_qty TYPE menge_d,
    lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

  " -------------------------------------------------------
  " Step 1: Must first copy complete input to output (Cloud BAdI standard practice)
  " -------------------------------------------------------
  MOVE-CORRESPONDING delivery_document_in TO delivery_document_out.
  delivery_document_items_out = delivery_document_items_in.

  " -------------------------------------------------------
  " Step 2: Header organization data filter
  " Only applies to Sales Org 1310 / DC 20 / Division 00
  " -------------------------------------------------------
  *CHECK delivery_document_in-salesorganization = lc_vkorg.

  " -------------------------------------------------------
  " Step 3: Loop through delivery line items
  " -------------------------------------------------------
  LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item).

    " Skip line items without reference sales order
    *CHECK ls_item-referencesddocument IS NOT INITIAL.
    *CHECK ls_item-referencesddocumentitem IS NOT INITIAL.

    " Read order quantity from sales order line item
    SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
      FROM i_salesorderitem
      WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
        AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
      INTO @LV_so_qty.
    " Read material stock quantity
    SELECT SINGLE matlwrhsstkqtyinmatlbaseunit
      FROM i_stockquantitycurrentvalue_2( p_displaycurrency = 'CNY' )
      WHERE product = @LS_item-material
      INTO @LV_stock.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      CONTINUE. " No corresponding sales order line item found, skip
    ENDIF.

    " Compare stock quantity with sales order quantity
    IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
      " Update custom field
      delivery_document_out-yy1_message_dlh = 'Warning: Insufficient stock quantity for this material.'.
    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

5. CDS Views Used

During implementation, we used the following CDS Views to retrieve the required data:

- I_SalesOrderItem: Used to get the order quantity of sales order line items, queried by sales order number and line item number
- I_StockQuantityCurrentValue_2: Used to get the current stock quantity of materials, providing real-time stock information for materials at different storage locations, which is ideal for stock checking scenarios

6. Testing and Validation

After testing, the above code successfully meets the requirements:

- When creating outbound deliveries in the frontend APP, if stock is insufficient, a Warning message is displayed

 

- When creating outbound deliveries via API, the custom field value is updated, and the stock shortage message can be seen in the Response's custom field

 

Conclusion

By reading this article and several similar scenario blogs I've written previously, I hope you now understand how to implement stock quantity checks through custom logic when creating outbound deliveries, and how to return appropriate messages in different scenarios.

Follow this link to see more questions and blog posts related to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Sales: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales - SAP Community

If you have any suggestions or ideas about this article, please feel free to share them in the comments section. I look forward to exchanging ideas with you. Also, please follow my personal account, and I hope to see you in the next blog post!

entry.title: Ariba Sourcing Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP – End-to-End Business Process
entry.content:
Introduction

Organizations often spend considerable effort managing supplier quotations, evaluating bids, and manually creating purchasing documents. By integrating SAP Ariba Sourcing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP, businesses can automate the complete sourcing lifecycle—from creating a Request for Quotation (RFQ) to automatically generating a Purchase Order after supplier award.

In this blog, we demonstrate the end-to-end business process using the Automation of Sourcing with SAP Ariba Sourcing (Scope Item 4BL). The process shown here represents a simplified version of the standard 4BL business process. For complete configuration and setup instructions, please refer to the official Scope Item 4BL documentation.

The blog is built as a series of 2 parts and present the key highlights for:

  • Ariba Sourcing Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP – End-to-End Business Process
  • Technical configuration for integrating Ariba Sourcing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP

Business Process Overview

The sourcing process begins in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP, where a purchaser creates an RFQ. The RFQ is automatically transferred to SAP Ariba Sourcing, allowing strategic buyers to conduct the sourcing event, collect supplier responses, evaluate bids, and award the business to the selected supplier.

Once the supplier is awarded, SAP automatically synchronizes the result back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, where the corresponding purchasing document is created without manual intervention.

End-to-End Process Flow

Step 1: Create and Publish the RFQ

A purchaser creates a Request for Quotation (RFQ) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud using the External Sourcing Requirement option and publishes it.
This automatically creates a corresponding Sourcing Request in SAP Ariba Sourcing.

Step 2: Review and Approve the Sourcing Request

A Strategic Buyer logs into SAP Ariba Sourcing and:

  • Searches for the newly created sourcing request.
  • Completes the Prepare Sourcing Request task (if applicable).
  • Approves the sourcing request.
  • Opens the associated sourcing project.

This step ensures the sourcing request is ready for supplier collaboration.

Step 3: Publish the Sourcing Project

The Strategic Buyer reviews the sourcing project details and publishes the sourcing event, making it available for invited suppliers.
The sourcing event now becomes active for supplier participation.

Step 4: Supplier Participation

Suppliers log into SAP Ariba and perform the following activities:

  • Review the sourcing event.
